MINI MINUTES:
Specially Appearing DEFENDANTS MAXXVIN LLC, AN OHIO LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY and MAXXVIN LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Motion To Quash.

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Thomas Burg, Esq. (650.857.9500; tburg@bbslaw.com) of Bialson, Bergen & Schwab for Plaintiff.
Andrew Quan, Esq. (510-394-2466; andrew@quan.legal) for Specially Appearing Defendants, Maxxvin, LLC et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' (MAXXVIN LLC, AN OHIO LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, MAXXVIN LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY) motions to quash are continued to August 17, 2022, to allow for limited jurisdictional discovery.

The complaint sufficiently names Maxxvin LLC, the process server fixed the technical defects in the proofs of service, and the service statutes are liberally construed where there is actual notice. (See Pasadena Medi-Center Associates v. Superior Court (1973) 9 Cal.3d 773, 778.)

When jurisdiction is challenged by a nonresident defendant, the plaintiff bears the burden of proof to establish minimum contacts between the defendant and the forum state so as to justify personal jurisdiction. (See Edmunds v. Superior Court (1994) 24 Cal. App. 4th 221, 228; Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-1440.)

Plaintiff currently fails to show that either MAXXVIN LLC, AN OHIO LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY or MAXXVIN LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY has sufficient minimum contacts with California to warrant the exercise of jurisdiction or that they are subject to the forum selection clause in the Master Subscription Agreement. Plaintiff is entitled to jurisdictional discovery to attempt to establish jurisdiction over movants. (See Goehring v. Superior Court (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 894, 911 ["A plaintiff is generally entitled to conduct discovery with regard to a jurisdictional issue before a court rules on a motion to quash."].)

Plaintiff may propound 15 special interrogatories to each movant regarding their contacts with California and relationship, if any, between themselves and plaintiff. Plaintiff may also depose each movant's person most knowledgeable for up to three hours regarding those issues.

Plaintiff's supplemental opposition shall be filed and served by August 3, 2022. Defendants' reply shall be filed and served by August 10, 2022.
